text: During the robotic right hemicolectomy, the surgeon uses a robotic stapler to transect the terminal ileum. The stapler's sensor ensures it is not fired across excessively thick tissue, and a yellow bar indicates the cutting action after staples are applied.

The surgeon uses a vessel sealer to take down the mesentery, then attaches the terminal ileum to the transverse colon with a silk suture. An enterotomy is created in both the end of the small bowel and the transverse colon, with Firefly imaging confirming adequate blood supply to both sections.

The surgeon fires the stapler to complete the side-to-side functional end-to-end anastomosis between the terminal ileum and transverse colon. The enterotomy is closed with a running 3-0 PDS suture, starting at either end to prevent leakage. Intercorporeal suturing is performed to avoid narrowing of the iliocolic anastomosis. An additional row of Lambert sutures is placed, and the small bowel defect in the mesentery is closed with 2-0 Vicryl suture.

The surgeon uses Firefly imaging to confirm adequate blood supply to the transverse colon and ileum at the anastomosis site. The specimen is removed using an endoscopic retrieval bag through a small incision.
